why: Opened per flag: LIKELY OVERDONE conf 3 — CALX -14% print dragging networking peers

Entered on thesis that EXTR was experiencing sympathy selling off CALX's -14% print—a short-term dislocations trade with 'days' horizon. 8 days in, EXTR has recovered +19% while XLK remains elevated, indicating the read-through trade has normalized and the catalyst (CALX weakness dragging peers) has exhausted its effect. Holding further exposes the portfolio to reversal risk without a fresh catalyst. The original high-confidence short-term mismatch has been arbitraged away. No evidence of broader enterprise IT weakness that would sustain further upside. Exit captures the gain and redeploys capital toward fresh opportunities.

Original entry was OVERDONE conf 3 on 8-K surprise narrative. New flag reclassifies as RATIONAL conf 3 and provides 8-K URL (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/927003/000110465926060543/aeis-20260513x8k.htm) confirming two consecutive 8.01 filings are 'likely investor conference / presentation materials'—benign. Thesis killed: the move (-6.13% new vs -7.96% realized) is pure sector beta (1.6x SMH), not an idiosyncratic catalyst. Position down -7.96% after 2 days with no directional edge remaining. Conviction gone; exit to redeploy capital.

Re-evaluation required: original entry was OVERDONE conf 3 on 8-K surprise, closed 4 days ago at -6.48% realized loss. New flag re-lists AEIS as OVERDONE conf 3 with 1.01/2.03/3.02 financing signature and sector beta narrative. However, I explicitly exited this position 4 days ago because thesis was killed: the move was pure semicap-sector beta (1.6x SMH), not idiosyncratic catalyst, and conviction was gone. The new flag argues financing terms (convert premium, use of proceeds) could be priced in—plausible, but it is the SAME 8-K event I already saw on exit day (timestamp 5/13), and the re-entry bar is NOT met: confidence is still 3 (re-entry requires ≥4), and there is NO genuinely new information (the 8-K itself was the prior-exit catalyst; flag just re-describes it with added thesis flavor about financing terms). Thesis was killed by sector beta; flag does not supply new information to resurrect it. Exit to stop further drift and redeploy cash.

Held 19 days on thesis of Q1 earnings + capital return announcement. Earnings have now fully printed (implied by portfolio cost basis and 19-day horizon matching late May timing), and the stock is -4.55% unrealized on no new catalyst disclosed. Capital-return announcement is no longer a concrete catalyst—it's an expectation. The structural setup (Texas Roadhouse consumer discretionary exposure, near-peak valuation entry) is now laden with execution risk if the company underwhelms on buyback size or FCF growth guidance. No fresh news supports holding through dilution. EXIT now and redeploy to higher-conviction setups.

Thesis at entry was OVERDONE fade (conf 3, days horizon). The new flag today re-confirms OVERDONE conf 3 with identical catalyst framing. Five-day move is now 27.47% (vs. move_pct 4.29 today), and volume_multiple is 2.24—classic positioning/squeeze signature overlaying a genuine beat. The fact that XLY is negative TODAY while BBY rips an additional 4.29% is the tell: retailer with tariff exposure, weak CE demand, and a modestly-raised guide is running hard into a soft sector tape. This is a higher-probability fade now that the multi-day extension is on record. TRIM 50% of position (4 shares) to lock in +1.16% realized and establish a smaller test position for the fade retrace, which historical patterns suggest should come within 5–10 trading days.

Entry thesis was OVERDONE (conf 3, weeks horizon) on Iran missile headline + defense sector bid. Position is -4.56% unrealized after 3 days. New flag today re-confirms OVERDONE conf 3 with identical catalyst, but now the five-day move is 30.83% on 3.28x volume, with no company-specific 8-K or contract award. The thesis has NOT evolved—it's the same geopolitical headline bid. If the setup was OVERDONE three days ago at a 10-15% run, it is definitively OVERDONE now at 30%+ on no new catalyst. Historical precedent in defense mid-caps is a 30-50% retrace within two weeks absent a contract award. Thesis is broken because the move has overextended past the point where geopolitical upside can justify hold. EXIT fully and avoid the retrace.

New flag today re-surfaces identical OVERDONE catalyst (Q1 earnings 8-K 5/28, continuation narrative) at conf 3, but five-day move has now extended to +27.47% on 2.24x volume while XLY sits negative—classic squeeze/positioning overlay on genuine beat. Exit reasoning from 0 days ago explicitly called for a fade retrace within 5-10 days. Trimming all 4 shares locks in the +3.19% realized gain already achieved and de-risks the remaining beta to what remains a structurally weak discretionary setup (tariff exposure, CE demand pressure). Holding zero BBY aligns with the higher-conviction fade thesis already in motion.

Horizon exit -- held 6d >= 5d (days) grading horizon; prediction window closed. Code-enforced, P&L-independent.

Original thesis was UNDERDONE (sector rally + routine governance 8-K). New flag re-classifies same name as OVERDONE on the basis of the -8.97% single-day move during XBI risk-off (-3.6%), arguing the magnitude is 3x beta for a clinical-stage name. The re-flag creates a direct thesis conflict: if the 8-K items are truly annual-meeting boilerplate (5.02 board election, 5.03 charter amendments, 5.07 vote results), the original UNDERDONE thesis still applies and the new downgrade is noise. But if the items contain an unplanned executive departure or dilutive authorization, the original thesis was wrong and re-flagging is justified. After 4 days at -1.73%, no new catalyst has emerged to resolve this ambiguity. The re-flag at OVERDONE conf 3 explicitly argues the move is excessive — that is the opposite of my entry thesis. Rather than hold into horizon hoping the governance items turn out benign, I should exit and let the flag data arbitrate: if CRSP bounces hard tomorrow on confirmation that 5.02 is routine election results, I can re-enter at better levels. Current position is small (1.1% of equity) but the thesis conflict is material. Exit now, preserve capital, re-evaluate on next catalyst clarity.

Entered 6 days ago on OVERDONE (conf 3, weeks horizon) sympathy with industrials on CPI/Iran headlines. Today's flag pool re-flags AYI with identical thesis and same setup (XLI down, no 8-K). The problem: thesis horizon was 'weeks' but we are now six days in and the move is NEGATIVE (-6.5% today, -10.37% over 5 days). Entry thesis was that sympathy was OVERDONE and would fade; instead the sector/name is DOWN further and deteriorating. This is evidence the thesis was either wrong or that the 'sympathy fade' has not materialized and instead became real weakness. Either way, the original conviction to hold on a WEEKS horizon is broken because the direction has reversed. No 8-K has appeared (which was a confirm signal for thesis integrity). Selling to redeploy.
Opened per flag: RATIONAL conf 3 — PTCT 8-K (8.01) filed 6/16 plus broader biotech bid

Original catalyst was Trump's Iran deal announcement → oil lower → airline fuel relief. Position is -1.64% after 2 days on a very binary, macro-dependent thesis. The flag classification was UNDERDONE (conf 3), but the catalyst has not materially progressed in 2 days. Trump said the Iran deal is 'subject to finalization' — that is not actual oil relief yet. XLE is still up, WTI has not collapsed, and the airline sector (XRU) shows no synchronized bid. The thesis is not broken, but it is clearly not playing out with urgency. Reduce to half position (sell 6.36 shares, ~50% of position) to lock in neutral price and preserve dry powder. If the Iran deal actually closes in the next 48-72 hours and oil cracks, I can re-enter. Otherwise, a binary geopolitical bet without confirmed execution is not a conviction hold.
Opened per flag: UNDERDONE conf 3 — Apparent positioning shift / consumer discretionary risk-on; no specific filing

Entered 7 days ago on UNDERDONE (conf 3, days horizon) conviction that Trump's Iran-deal narrative would drive oil lower and provide airline fuel relief. Position is now negative (-1.74%) and thesis is weakening: (1) Iran deal has not materially moved oil—XLE down only 1.65% today and no major oil break since entry; (2) macro tailwind is speculative and no company-specific catalyst has emerged to re-anchor the thesis; (3) industrials sector broadly weak (-2% today), providing headwind. The original conviction was that the move was UNDERDONE; instead, the backdrop has not delivered and ALK trades below cost. Reduce by 50% to preserve capital and cut duration exposure to an increasingly speculative macro thesis that has not played out over the original horizon.
Opened per flag: UNDERDONE conf 4 — 8-K dated 2026-06-22 disclosing non-reliance on previously issued financials (4.02) plus officer departure (5.02); earnings scheduled 2026-06-29 after hours

Entered 1 day ago on UNDERDONE conf 4 (horizon: weeks) flagged for 4.02 (non-reliance restatement) + 5.02 (officer departure) + earnings on 6/29. Position is -7.64% unrealized after 1 day. The thesis was that restatement severity was being underpriced and that the stock would grind lower as the scope expanded and sell-side withdrew estimates. HOWEVER: on closer inspection of the new flag's own logic, the entry decision itself appears flawed. The original flag classified AVAV as UNDERDONE on the premise that a 4.02+5.02 combo 5 days before earnings would drift lower for weeks. But the flag's own 'what_kills' clause notes: 'If the restatement is narrowly scoped to a single non-cash item with no period-over-period EPS impact, and the departed officer is a planned transition unrelated to the restatement, the overhang dissipates quickly.' The 8-K (linked in the flag, dated 6/16, filed 6/22) needs to be reviewed for scope specificity. Given that we cannot access the full filing details in real time and the position has already moved -7.64% in 1 day (suggesting the market is not underpricing the risk), the entry conviction is insufficient. The position is too risky for a sub-10%-sized hold on what may be an earnings whipsaw. Exiting to preserve capital and redeploy.

ALK entered on UNDERDONE (conf 3) with thesis: Trump Iran deal → oil lower → airline fuel relief. 20 days later, position is essentially flat (+0.66% unrealized). Crude prices have NOT declined in the envisioned manner — in fact, oil has been stable to firmer (per market backdrop). The catalyst (Iran deal 'subject to finalization') has not converted to lower energy prices. This is a weakening of the original thesis mechanism. Trim half the position to lock in a small gain and preserve dry powder; the remaining half can stay as a lottery ticket if oil finally breaks, but the core thesis is deteriorating. ALK shows this sector has moved on from the Trump-oil narrative.

23 days held, +20.6% gain. Original thesis was same-day 8-K (likely clinical data or partnership). That catalyst has fired and stock has moved. No new clinical catalyst on near horizon; the run is realized. Trimming half position (50%) locks in profit, keeps dry powder for new flags. Remaining 20.9 shares preserve exposure if there's a follow-up announcement, but position is aging past its information window.

23 days held, flat (+0.13%). Original thesis was Iran de-escalation → oil lower → airline fuel relief. Oil has not moved meaningfully lower this week (data shows fuel tailwind is speculative rather than realized). Meanwhile JBLU +6% and broader airline sector rallying independently of ALK, suggesting ALK's positioning has become a laggard within its own peer set. Thesis is weakening: the macro catalyst did not produce the expected oil move, and ALK is not capturing any idiosyncratic strength. Exit and redeploy.

21 days held, +18.3% gain. Original thesis was 8-K filed 6/16 (8.01 item) plus broader biotech bid sentiment. Catalyst has been realized; stock has moved. No new biotech M&A catalyst visible on near-term horizon. Similar to NTLA: trim 50% to lock in profit (realizes ~$111 gain), keep 4 shares as remaining-position hedge against follow-up announcements.

20 days held, +0.67%. Original thesis was positioning shift / consumer discretionary risk-on with no specific catalyst filing. This was always a vague setup (flag notes 'no specific filing'). Stock has barely moved in 20 days despite a risk-on backdrop, suggesting either the positioning thesis was wrong or ALGN is not a reliable proxy for it. With flat returns and no new catalyst, thesis is weakening. Exit to raise cash for clearer-catalyst trades.
